Ledgely — Small Business Systems

Clear answers, simple tools, and templates for small business owners. Step-by-step guides for EIN, ITIN, business setup, expense tracking, invoicing, and financial clarity.

Free Guides

What is an EIN. Who needs an EIN. EIN Application Preparation. What is an ITIN. ITIN Application Checklist. How to register a small business. How to track business expenses. What is a profit and loss statement. Cash flow basics for small businesses.

Free Tools

Monthly Profit Estimator. Business Expense Tracker. Cash Flow Planner. Invoice Generator.

Pro Systems

Business Starter System. Financial Control System. Client and Invoice Management System.

Documentation

Browse every guide. Use search or pick a cluster to narrow down.

Clusters

EIN

  • 3 min read

    What is an EIN?

    An EIN is a free 9-digit tax ID from the IRS — required for business bank accounts, payroll, and taxes. Here's what it is and how to get one.

  • 3 min read

    Who needs an EIN?

    Not every small business is required to have an EIN — but most benefit from one. Find out if you need one and how getting one protects your SSN.

  • 6 min read

    EIN Application Preparation Checklist (Business Tax ID)

    Everything you need before applying for an EIN: legal name, business structure, responsible party, and state info. Prepare in 5 minutes, apply in 10.

  • 4 min read

    Common EIN mistakes to avoid

    The most common EIN application errors that cause rejections or delays — and exactly what to do instead before submitting to the IRS.

ITIN

  • 3 min read

    What is an ITIN?

    An ITIN lets non-US citizens file federal taxes and open US accounts without a Social Security Number. Plain-English explanation of what it is and who needs one.

  • 3 min read

    Who needs an ITIN?

    Non-residents, immigrants, and foreign nationals filing US taxes often need an ITIN. Find out who qualifies and whether it's the right tax ID for your situation.

  • 6 min read

    ITIN Application Checklist (Individual Tax ID)

    A step-by-step checklist of documents needed for IRS Form W-7 — the ITIN application — including accepted ID types, where to mail it, and what to expect.

  • 3 min read

    ITIN vs EIN: what's the difference?

    ITIN is for individuals who can't get an SSN. EIN is for businesses. A clear side-by-side comparison so you know exactly which tax ID applies to you.

Business Setup

  • 5 min read

    How do I register a small business?

    The correct order to legally register a small business in the US: structure, state filing, EIN, bank account, and licenses — step by step, in plain English.

  • 4 min read

    Required startup documents for a small business

    The formation documents, tax IDs, licenses, and financial records every small business needs on file from day one — with a printable checklist.

  • 4 min read

    Basic compliance checklist for small businesses

    Annual reports, license renewals, payroll filings, and tax deadlines — the recurring compliance requirements small businesses must track to stay in good standing.

  • 5 min read

    Tax Setup Document Organizer (Personal & Business)

    A clean checklist to help you gather and organize the documents you'll typically need for personal and business tax setup.

  • 5 min read

    LLC vs sole proprietorship: which is right for your small business?

    A plain-English comparison of the two most common small business structures — what each one means, costs, and when to choose one over the other.

  • 4 min read

    What is a DBA (doing business as) and do you need one?

    A clear explanation of trade names, assumed names, and DBAs — what they are, when you need one, and how to file.

Financial Systems

  • 6 min read

    How do I track business expenses?

    A simple expense tracking system for small business owners — what categories to use, where to keep records, and how to make tax time far less painful.

  • 4 min read

    What is a profit and loss statement?

    A P&L shows your revenue, expenses, and net profit for a period. Learn what every line means, how to read one, and how to run it for your small business.

  • 4 min read

    Cash flow basics for small businesses

    Cash flow is not the same as profit — and the gap is why businesses fail. Learn how to track timing, invoice faster, and keep enough cash on hand.

  • 4 min read

    How to open a business bank account

    What you need, where to go, and why separating your business and personal finances from day one saves significant time and money.

  • 6 min read

    Self-employed tax deductions: what small business owners can write off

    A plain-English list of the most common and valuable tax deductions available to self-employed individuals and small business owners.

  • 5 min read

    Quarterly estimated taxes for small businesses

    How self-employed individuals and small business owners pay taxes during the year — and how to avoid underpayment penalties.

  • 3 min read

    What is a W-9 form and when do you need one?

    A plain-English explanation of IRS Form W-9 — what it is, who fills it out, who requests it, and when it's required.

  • 4 min read

    Business mileage deduction: how to track and claim it

    How the IRS business mileage deduction works, what counts as a deductible trip, and the simplest way to keep a compliant mileage log.

Money & Profit

  • 5 min read

    How Small Businesses Lose Money Without Noticing

    The silent leaks draining your profit each month, and a simple way to spot them before they grow.

Sales & Clients

  • 7 min read

    A Basic Client Follow-Up System to Avoid Lost Sales

    Most lost sales aren't lost — they're forgotten. A simple follow-up system so no opportunity slips through.

  • 5 min read

    How to write a business invoice that gets paid faster

    What to include on a professional invoice, how to structure it, and practical steps that reduce the time between sending and getting paid.

Marketing & Growth

  • 5 min read

    How to get your first 10 customers

    Your first 10 customers almost never come from ads or social media. A practical, no-budget plan using direct outreach to the people and communities you already know.

  • 5 min read

    Free marketing channels for a new small business

    The free, sustainable marketing channels that actually work for small businesses without a budget or marketing team.

  • 4 min read

    How to write an offer people actually respond to

    Most offers fail because they're vague. This framework shows you how to write a clear, specific offer that converts real conversations into paying customers.

  • 4 min read

    Word of mouth — how to build a referral system for free

    Word of mouth doesn't need to stay random. A simple referral system turns your best customers into a steady source of new ones.

  • 4 min read

    Simple social media for small businesses with no budget

    A no-budget social media plan that takes 30 minutes a week and works without ads, agencies, or daily posting.

  • 5 min read

    How to price your services (and stop undercharging)

    Stop guessing on price. A practical method for calculating what to charge based on real costs, market rate, and client value — so you stop leaving money on the table.
